title: GuardianClaw Pattern Registry
description: Neutral source of truth for named pattern lists consumed by both the Python SDK (re module) and the TypeScript core (RegExp). Schema v2 supports two kinds: 'indicators' (lowercased substring lists, matched with caller-side text.lower() / toLowerCase()) and 'regex' (regex source strings emitted as r-strings in Python and as regex literals in TS). For 'regex' kind, the Py and JS engines differ in some constructs; only patterns that are equivalent in both engines belong in the registry. Divergent patterns stay hand-written on each side until the parity audit (Frente C.3) reconciles them.

name.description: Stable identifier used as the pattern-sync marker name in generated source files.

flags.description: Regex flags, applied to every pattern in the family. Subset that maps cleanly between Python's re and JavaScript's RegExp: i (IGNORECASE), m (MULTILINE), s (DOTALL), u (UNICODE — JS-only flag, no-op in Py except for explicit unicode handling), x (Py-only verbose). Default empty. Only meaningful when kind='regex'.

items.description: Lowercased string. Matching is caller-defined (typically substring on text.lower()).
Regex source. Must use only constructs that have identical semantics in Python's re and JavaScript's RegExp. The generator does not transform — the same string is emitted to both runtimes.
